long_term_goal: The DxD design team composes all 1440×900 layouts using the web tool. Every layout produced is token-correct, rule-compliant, and spec-ready. Engineering receives structured handoff data rather than redlined Figma frames. The tool is the source of truth for the fragment system.
sprint_questions: Can we build a drag-to-resize grid interaction that snaps to the 12px structural grid and feels fast enough to replace ad-hoc Figma frame arrangement?
Will designers adopt per-edge connectivity toggles as a natural part of layout composition, or will they revert to applying tokens manually?
Can we produce a per-slot spec sheet (token names, pixel values, connectivity state) that an engineer can implement without supplementary documentation?
key_question_answered: All three questions were validated as the right questions to test; none are answered yet — they are the success criteria for the implementation sprint.
key_decision: Proceeded with the interactive web tool MVP scoped to drag-to-resize grid composition, per-edge connectivity toggles, and a live spec panel — all other features deferred.
elias_dissent: Elias challenged the team to define a measurable win condition before building. Partially upheld: win condition added (2-minute, no-reference-guide, rule-compliant layout). His broader scepticism about build justification was overruled — the Figma per-corner-radius limitation is a hard technical blocker, not a preference.
next_action: Define and align on the Zustand state model data shape and implement the getSlotRadii pure function with unit tests before writing any interaction code.
open_questions: What are the exact safe area pixel dimensions for the EDL header (top) and utility bar (bottom) on Electra?
What are the container widths for Hoya (15") and Madeline (8")?
Should ratio snapping support arbitrary values (e.g. 3:7) or only the defined presets?
Should the connectivity affordance appear on hover only, or always be visible between adjacent slots?
Is Framer Motion's spring physics configurable enough for elastic snap, or do we need a custom spring?
